# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"
          cache: pip

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi
          if [ -f requirements-dev.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements-dev.txt; fi

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          python -m pip install pytest
          pytest -q

  deploy:
    name: Deploy
    needs: test
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"
          cache: pip

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi

      - name: Build package
        run: |
          python -m pip install build
          python -m build

      - name: Deploy
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }}
          port: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PORT || 22 }}
          script: |
            set -e
            cd "${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }}"
            git fetch --all
            git reset --hard origin/main
            if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt; fi
            if [ -n "${{ secrets.DEPLOY_RESTART_CMD }}" ]; then
              ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_RESTART_CMD }}
            fi
